sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_strings (id INT PRIMARY KEY, s1 VARCHAR(255), s2 VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO test_strings (id, s1, s2) VALUES (1, 'MonetDB', 'DB3');
INSERT INTO test_strings (id, s1, s2) VALUES (2, 'Test', 'Testing');
SELECT id, levenshtein(s1, s2, 1, 1) AS distance FROM test_strings WHERE levenshtein(s1, s2, 1, 1) <> 3;
CREATE TABLE test_ips (id INT PRIMARY KEY, ip1 INET, ip2 INET);
INSERT INTO test_ips (id, ip1, ip2) VALUES (1, inet '192.168.1.5', inet '192.168.1.5');
INSERT INTO test_ips (id, ip1, ip2) VALUES (2, inet '192.168.1.5', inet '192.168.1.4');
SELECT id FROM test_ips WHERE levenshtein(CAST(ip1 AS VARCHAR), CAST(ip2 AS VARCHAR), 1, 1) <> levenshtein(CAST(ip1 AS VARCHAR), '192.168.1.5', 1, 1);
CREATE TABLE test_advanced (id INT PRIMARY KEY, text_column VARCHAR(255), ip_column INET);
INSERT INTO test_advanced (id, text_column, ip_column) VALUES (1, 'Sample', inet '192.168.1.1');
INSERT INTO test_advanced (id, text_column, ip_column) VALUES (2, NULL, inet '192.168.1.2');
SELECT id, levenshtein(text_column, 'Sample', 1, 1) AS distance FROM test_advanced WHERE (text_column IS NULL OR text_column <> 'Sample');
CREATE TABLE test_join (id INT PRIMARY KEY, reference_id INT, value VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO test_join (id, reference_id, value) VALUES (1, 1, 'One');
INSERT INTO test_join (id, reference_id, value) VALUES (2, 1, 'Two');
SELECT tj.id, tj.value FROM test_join tj JOIN test_strings ts ON levenshtein(ts.s1, 'One', 1, 1) <> ts.id WHERE ts.id = tj.reference_id;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_strings WHERE levenshtein(s1, s2, 1, 1) BETWEEN 1 AND 5 HAVING COUNT(*) <> 0;
